heading: Educational seminars to improve efforts to classify and appraise imported articles, to improve trade enforcement efforts, and to otherwise facilitate legitimate international trade
label: 4313 Educational seminars to improve efforts to classify and appraise imported articles, to improve trade enforcement efforts, and to otherwise facilitate legitimate international trade

children.text: improve the ability of personnel of U.S. Customs and Border Protection to classify and appraise articles imported into the United States in accordance with the customs and trade laws of the United States;
improve the trade enforcement efforts of personnel of U.S. Customs and Border Protection and personnel of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement; and

otherwise improve the ability and effectiveness of personnel of U.S. Customs and Border Protection and personnel of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement to facilitate legitimate international trade.

children.label: A
children.text: Conducting a physical inspection of an article imported into the United States, including testing of samples of the article, to determine if the article is mislabeled in the manifest or other accompanying documentation.

children.label: B
children.text: Reviewing the manifest and other accompanying documentation of an article imported into the United States to determine if the country of origin of the article listed in the manifest or other accompanying documentation is accurate.

children.label: C
children.text: Customs valuation.

children.label: D
children.text: Industry supply chains and other related matters as determined to be appropriate by the Commissioner.

children.label: A
children.text: Collection of countervailing duties assessed under subtitle A of title VII of the Tariff Act of 1930 ( et seq.) and antidumping duties assessed under subtitle B of title VII of the Tariff Act of 1930 ( et seq.).19 U.S.C. 167119 U.S.C. 1673

children.label: B
children.text: Addressing evasion of duties on imports of textiles.

children.label: C
children.text: Protection of intellectual property rights.

children.label: D
children.text: Enforcement of child labor laws.

children.heading: Approval of Commissioner and Director
children.label: 3 Approval of Commissioner and Director
children.text: The instruction and related instructional materials at each educational seminar carried out under this section shall be subject to the approval of the Commissioner and the Director.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Commissioner shall establish a process to solicit, evaluate, and select interested parties in the private sector for purposes of assisting in providing instruction and related instructional materials described in subsection (b) at each educational seminar carried out under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: availability and usefulness;

children.label: B
children.text: the volume, value, and incidence of mislabeling or misidentification of origin of imported articles; and

children.label: C
children.text: other appropriate criteria established by the Commissioner.

children.heading: Public availability
children.label: 3 Public availability
children.text: The Commissioner and the Director shall publish in the Federal Register a detailed description of the process established under paragraph (1) and the criteria established under paragraph (2).

children.heading: Special rule for antidumping and countervailing duty orders
children.label: d Special rule for antidumping and countervailing duty orders

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Commissioner shall give due consideration to carrying out an educational seminar under this section in whole or in part to improve the ability of personnel of U.S. Customs and Border Protection to enforce a countervailing or antidumping duty order issued under section 706 or 736 of the Tariff Act of 1930 ( or 1673e) upon the request of a petitioner in an action underlying such countervailing or antidumping duty order.19 U.S.C. 1671e

children.heading: Interested party
children.label: 2 Interested party
children.text: A petitioner described in paragraph (1) shall be treated as an interested party in the private sector for purposes of the requirements of this section.

children.heading: Performance standards
children.label: e Performance standards
children.text: The Commissioner and the Director shall establish performance standards to measure the development and level of achievement of educational seminars carried out under this section.

children.heading: Reporting
children.label: f Reporting
children.text: Not later than , and annually thereafter, the Commissioner and the Director shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees a report on the effectiveness of educational seminars carried out under this section.September 30, 2016

children.heading: Director
children.label: 1 Director
children.text: The term “Director” means the Director of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement.

children.heading: United States
children.label: 2 United States
children.text: The term “United States” means the customs territory of the United States, as defined in General Note 2 to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

children.heading: U.S. Customs and Border Protection personnel
children.label: 3 U.S. Customs and Border Protection personnel
children.text: The term “U.S. Customs and Border Protection personnel” means import specialists, auditors, and other appropriate employees of the U.S. Customs and Border Protection.

children.heading: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement personnel
children.label: 4 U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement personnel
children.text: The term “U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement personnel” means Homeland Security Investigations Directorate personnel and other appropriate employees of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement.
